spec_match.rationale: The demo effectively showcases using image(<color>) for list-style-image to create solid color bullets.

interactive.rationale: The color picker provides a real-time interactive demonstration of generating dynamic image() bullets with matching code display.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The parent blurb matches the list bullet concept perfectly.

edge_cases.rationale: It provides a useful comparison between standard ::marker coloring and the new list-style-image: image(<color>) approach.

references.rationale: Includes the required ChromeStatus reference link in the see also section.

design_system.rationale: Adheres to the design system, using CSS variables for standard and custom brand colors.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate list-style-type fallback
openQuestions.detail: The demo relies on the default browser bullet (disc) as a fallback if `image()` is not supported. It would be good to explicitly show how to define a fallback `list-style-type` (e.g., `square`) in the CSS rules and code examples to ensure consistent rendering across older browsers.

summary: A well-structured demo that clearly demonstrates list-style-image: image(<color>) with a real-time color picker. The comparison with standard ::marker styling is highly educational, though it could explicitly show list-style-type fallbacks.
